expectations.text: Produces both deliverables — a markdown behavior spec (.md) and a Cucumber .feature file
expectations.evidence: Output dir contains transaction-csv-export-behavior-spec.md and transaction-csv-export.feature.

expectations.text: The .feature file is well-formed Gherkin (Feature declaration, Scenario blocks, Given/When/Then steps)
expectations.evidence: transaction-csv-export.feature has a 'Feature:' + 'Background:' header and 29 well-formed Scenario blocks; scenarios identical to the .md.

expectations.text: Happy-path scenarios are tagged @happy-path and match the CSV export flow described in the prompt
expectations.evidence: 2 @happy-path scenarios: synchronous date-range export with download, and a large export run as a background job — matches and sensibly extends the prompt's flow.

expectations.text: The spec includes a coverage checklist enumerating all 14 deviation taxonomy categories
expectations.evidence: Section 9 Coverage Checklist has all 14 numbered category rows, each marked Yes with scenario counts.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios span at least 8 distinct deviation categories
expectations.evidence: All 14 distinct category tags present across 27 deviation scenarios.

expectations.text: Deviation scenario Then-steps specify concrete graceful product behavior, not merely that an error occurred
expectations.evidence: e.g. connectivity loss: user told it did not complete, range preserved, 'no partial or corrupt CSV presented as complete'; blocked download: explicit manual download link offered.

expectations.text: Genuine product decisions are tagged @assumption and consolidated into a Flagged Assumptions section
expectations.evidence: 7 scenarios tagged @assumption with '# Assumption:' comments; Section 8 consolidates 9 assumptions (A1–A9) as a table with what to confirm.

expectations.text: The .md spec includes the narrative sections Problem & Intent, Actors, Glossary, Preconditions, and Scope
expectations.evidence: Sections 1–5 are Problem & Intent, Actors, Glossary, Preconditions & Assumptions, Scope.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios cover an empty date range, an invalid range (end before start), a large export, and connectivity loss mid-flow
expectations.evidence: 7.9 empty date range, 7.1 end-before-start, 7.10 oversized range as background job, 7.4 connectivity loss during generation — all present.
